headline_summary: U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran have escalated into a regional conflict with direct impacts on energy infrastructure and shipping.
Iran has declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and warned it will fire on any ship attempting to pass; multiple vessels have been hit.
Iranian missile attacks were reported across Gulf states hosting U.S. forces, prompting air-defence interceptions and disrupting daily life.
Major shipping lines have paused/redirected Suez/Bab el-Mandeb transits and suspended Hormuz crossings; war-risk surcharges and shelter instructions signal elevated maritime risk.
The U.S. Treasury has expanded Iran-related sanctions targeting a shadow-fleet and networks linked to petroleum sales and weapons programs.
risk_drivers: Strait of Hormuz closure threat and stated intent to fire on transiting ships
Direct attacks on tankers/merchant shipping in or near Hormuz
Missile exchanges affecting Gulf states hosting U.S. bases (Kuwait, Qatar, Bahrain, UAE)
Shipping reroutes away from Suez/Bab el-Mandeb plus Hormuz suspensions raising freight/insurance costs
Sanctions escalation that can tighten enforcement risk and constrain Iranian export logistics

Energy: oil_up_risk: sustained Hormuz disruption materially raises crude/LNG risk premium and physical supply uncertainty.
Higher tanker and container freight/insurance costs can lift delivered energy prices and widen regional differentials.
FX: risk_off_flows: heightened geopolitical risk tends to support USD and pressure EM FX, especially energy importers.
em_fx_pressure: shipping/energy price shock can worsen current accounts for import-dependent EMs.
Rates: rates_credit_spreads: higher energy-driven inflation risk can lift term premia and widen credit spreads; shipping/energy-linked credits face repricing.
Risk-off demand can temporarily lower safe-haven yields even as inflation risk rises (higher volatility).
Equities: risk_off_flows: global equities may de-rate; airlines/transport/chemicals vulnerable to fuel and freight costs.
Energy producers and some defense names can outperform in a higher-risk-premium regime.

sources.title: US and Israel launch "pre-emptive" attack against Iran
Iranian missiles shake Gulf cities after US, Israeli strikes on Iran
Iran vows to attack any ship trying to pass through Strait of Hormuz
Fuel tanker ablaze in Strait of Hormuz after drone strike, Iran Revolutionary Guards say
Shipping companies divert vessels around Cape of Good Hope after strikes on Iran
US expands sanctions on Iran over oil sales, weapons programs
Treasury Targets Iran's Shadow Fleet, Networks Supplying Ballistic Missile and ACW Programs

events.title: US and Israel launch pre-emptive strikes on Iran
events.implication: Marks escalation to direct interstate conflict; raises probability of retaliation against regional bases, shipping and energy infrastructure.

events.title: Iran fires missiles at Gulf cities after US-Israeli strikes
events.implication: Direct attacks on Gulf states increase regional spillover risk and elevate concerns about energy and logistics continuity.

events.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz closed and threatens ships attempting to transit
events.implication: Even partial closure can disrupt ~20% of global oil flows and LNG shipments, sharply lifting freight, insurance and oil risk premia.

events.title: Fuel tanker Athe Nova reported hit by drones and burning in Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Attack on a tanker reinforces physical risk to maritime flows; increases chance of shipping stand-down and insurance withdrawal.

events.title: Maersk pauses Trans-Suez sailings and major lines reroute around Cape; Hormuz crossings suspended
events.location_name: Suez Canal / Bab el-Mandeb / Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Reroutes extend voyage times, tighten vessel availability, and raise shipping costs; compounds energy market stress via logistics constraints.

events.title: US expands Iran-related sanctions targeting shadow fleet and supply networks
events.implication: Tightens enforcement risk around Iranian petroleum logistics and procurement; can redirect flows and increase compliance costs for shippers/traders.

links.from: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz closed and threatens ships attempting to transit
links.why: Hormuz is a critical chokepoint; closure threats and attacks directly raise risk premium for crude/LNG.

links.from: Fuel tanker Athe Nova reported hit by drones and burning in Strait of Hormuz
links.why: Demonstrates realized shipping attack risk, prompting stand-downs and higher insurance/freight.

links.from: Maersk pauses Trans-Suez sailings and major lines reroute around Cape; Hormuz crossings suspended
links.why: Visible supply-chain shock and higher freight costs tend to trigger broader risk aversion.

links.from: Iran fires missiles at Gulf cities after US-Israeli strikes
links.why: Regional escalation to Gulf states raises tail-risk perceptions across assets.
Risk-off episodes often weaken EM FX; energy-importers face extra pressure from higher oil and freight.
Oil shock can raise inflation risk and widen credit spreads via tighter financial conditions.
